headline: The pitfalls of Hungary's post-Orban transition
description: Riding a powerful wave of opposition to the outgoing government, Hungary's new prime minister, Péter Magyar, now has to balance the expectations of both left- and right-wing voters while clarifying his positions, especially regarding Ukraine.

headline: Virginie Despentes and over 130 writers quit publishing house after Bolloré fires CEO
Concerns mount over private credit in the United States
What is the controversial bill on antisemitism French MPs are debating?
description: MP Caroline Yadan has been accused of trying to silence criticism of Israel with her bill, which aims to 'combat new forms of antisemitism.' A petition against the text has gathered over 700,000 signatures.

headline: 'Why not try to remobilize a France that has faith in its future?'
description: To counter a collective sense of powerlessness, the worst poison for any democracy, political scientist François Godard proposes to build a platform restoring the State's capacity by rallying engineers and other driving forces across the country.

headline: At Lyon's Musée des Confluences, the Loeb donation celebrates Mali's living arts
description: More than 100 masks, puppets and instruments are displayed in a way that evokes the singing, dancing and movements that once accompanied ceremonies along the Niger River.

headline: Rapper Gims linked to international money laundering ring
description: Investigators uncovered nearly €8 million in suspicious transactions involving Gims, his inner circle, and an international criminal organization. The Congolese music star has been charged. He denies the accusations

The Curtis Yarvin conundrum: Should one debate with the far right?
description: The reactionary American blogger close to the Trumpian figures was invited to a symposium in Germany with prominent scholars. Some critics say the invitation signals that red lines have vanished from intellectual debate.

headline: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
description: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.

headline: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
description: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.
